body.title: CLOSED: Tests must clear their inbox messages on completion
body.result: conftest cc_inbox_test_tag now sweeps [test:] at session START (not only on teardown) — stale entries from killed sessions cleared on next run; commit 6323f53f
body.description:
Tests post prefixed messages to the CC inbox (e.g., 'pe_chain_block [T-test]') but don't clear them on completion. Currently 87 unread inbox messages are test blocks from Igor, blocking context visibility.

Add cleanup logic to test runners and pe_chain completion handlers to remove messages with test-identifying prefixes (pe_chain_block, pe_chain_escape, etc.) from the inbox when the test exits.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_inbox.py, wild_igor/igor/pe_chain/, wild_igor/tests/conftest.py or matching fixture cleanup hooks

**Design rules:** none apply

**Scope boundary:** IN: Add inbox.clear_by_prefix() or matching method; wire it to test cleanup on pass/fail/block. OUT: Restructuring inbox system; changing how tests post messages.

**Test plan:** Run a pe_chain test, observe message posted with 'pe_chain_block [T-xxx]' prefix. Verify on completion (pass/fail/block), message is deleted from inbox. Verify non-test messages persist.

**Pass condition:** After a test run completes, inbox contains zero messages with 'pe_chain_block' or 'pe_chain_escape' prefix that were posted during that test.
